import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut i = 0
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut start: Int = -1
  while i < n
    mut b = 0
    match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(v)
      b = v
    when None
      ()
    end
    let is_ws = b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 10 or b == 13
    if is_ws
      if start >= 0
        let tok = string.substring(s, start, i)
        mut num = 0
        match string.parse_int(tok)
        when Some(v)
          num = v
        when None
          ()
        end
        result = list.push(result, num)
        start = -1
      end
    else
      if start < 0
        start = i
      end
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if start >= 0
    let tok = string.substring(s, start, n)
    mut num = 0
    match string.parse_int(tok)
    when Some(v)
      num = v
    when None
      ()
    end
    result = list.push(result, num)
  end
  result
end

fn bsearch(_ xs: List<Int>, _ t: Int) -> Int
  mut lo = 0
  mut hi = xs.len()
  mut found: Int = -1
  while lo < hi
    let mid = (lo + hi) / 2
    mut v = 0
    match xs.get(mid)
    when Some(x)
      v = x
    when None
      ()
    end
    if v == t
      found = mid
      lo = hi
    else if v < t
      lo = mid + 1
    else
      hi = mid
    end
  end
  found
end

mut line1 = ""
match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  line1 = s
when None
  ()
end

mut line2 = ""
match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  line2 = s
when None
  ()
end

let xs = parse_ints(line1)
let trimmed = string.trim(line2)
mut t = 0
match string.parse_int(trimmed)
when Some(v)
  t = v
when None
  ()
end

let idx = bsearch(xs, t)
if idx < 0
  print("not_found")
else
  print("index=#{idx}")
end
